title: Catalyst Watch: SpaceX IPO, Apple's WWDC, CPI, and the World Cup kicks off
content:
[Wall Street New York stock exchange stock market]
alexsl

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - JUNE 8

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Seabridge Gold (SA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SA]) and Medical Properties Trust (MPT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MPT]) have seen options volatility increase over the last week. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Laser Photonics (LASE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LASE]), Solidion Technology (STI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STI]), and Netclass Technology (NTCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTCL]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Mountain Lake Acquisition (MLAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MLAC]) and ADC Therapeutics (ADCT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ADCT]). Short interest is elevated once again on Groupon (GRPN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GRPN]) and Vital Farms (VITL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VITL]). 
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Campbell's Company (CPB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CPB]), and Vail Resorts (MTN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MTN]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Alphabet (GOOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOG]) (GOOGL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOGL]), Occidental Petroleum (OXY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OXY]), Travelers (TRV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TRV]), and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSM]).
 	* IPO WATCH - SpaceX (SPCX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPCX]) will dominate IPO talk with the aerospace giant positioned to start trading on June 12 at a preset pricing level of $135 per share. The Elon Musk-led company is seeking to raise approximately $75B through the sale of 555.6M Class A shares priced at $135 each. At the proposed offering price, SpaceX would have a market value of roughly $1.77T. Other companies expected to price their IPOs during the week and begin to trade include Lannister Mining (DRIL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DRIL]), ERock (EROC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EROC]), and Forbright (FRBT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FRBT]).The analyst quiet period ends on Cerebras Systems (CBRS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CBRS]), Fervo Energy (FRVO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FRVO]), EagleRock Land (EROK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EROK]), and Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust (BXDC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BXDC]) to free up analysts to post ratings.
 	* ALL WEEK - The conference season heats up with the D. A. Davidson Technology & Consumer Conference, Goldman Sachs Global Healthcare Conference, Wells Fargo Industrials Conference, and Evercore ISI Consumer and Retail Conference all taking place.
 	* ALL WEEK - The FOMC blackout period begins in advance of the meeting on June 16-17.
 	* ALL DAY - Apple (AAPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AAPL]) will hold its WWDC software event, headlined by iOS 27, iPadOS 27, macOS 27, watchOS 27, tvOS 27, and visionOS 27 updates. The theme "All Systems Glow" points to Apple's biggest push yet into on-device AI, with an overhauled Siri and broader Apple Intelligence.
 	* 10:00 A.M. The Existing Home Sales report will be released by the National Association of Realtors.
 	* 12:00 P.M. Pegasystems (PEGA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PEGA]) will hold an in-person investor session at its annual PegaWorld user conference.
 	* 3:00 P.M. The New York Fed's Survey of Consumer Expectations report will be released.

TUESDAY - JUNE 9

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Casey's General Stores (CASY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CASY]), J.M. Smucker (SJM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SJM]), Cracker Barrel Old Country Store (CBRL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CBRL]), and SailPoint (SAIL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SAIL]). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Domo (DOMO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOMO]) and Titan Machinery (TITN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TITN]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Notable investor events include Cloudflare's (NET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NET]) Investor Day, and Tyler Technologies' (TYL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TYL]) Investor Day.
 	* ALL DAY - Datadog (DDOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DDOG]) will hold its annual flagship Datadog DASH conference for builders, engineers, and security teams, focused on observability, security, and AI in modern systems.

WEDNESDAY - JUNE 10

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Oracle (ORCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ORCL]), Chewy (CHWY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CHWY]), and Navan (NAVN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NAVN]).
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Kennedy-Wilson Holdings (KW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KW]) will vote on the acquisition offer from a consortium led by CEO William McMorrow and senior management, along with Fairfax Financial Holdings Limited (FFH:CA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FFH:CA]).
 	* ALL DAY - Notable investor events include Rubrik's (RBRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RBRK]) Analyst Day.
 	* ALL DAY - Anthropic (ANTHRO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ANTHRO]) is holding a Code w/Claude event in Tokyo.
 	* ALL DAY - Booking Holdings (BKNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BKNG]) CEO Glenn Fogel and Box (BOX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BOX]) CEO Aaron Levie will be speakers at the SEMAFOR Tech conference.
 	* 8:30 P.M. The Consumer Price Index report for May will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists expect the core inflation rate to bump up to 2.9%. The airfare component is anticipated to see a big jump.

THURSDAY - JUNE 11

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Adobe (ADBE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ADBE]), Lennar (LEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LEN]), and RH (RH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RH]).
 	* ALL DAY - The opening match of the World Cup takes place when Mexico hosts South Africa at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City. The 39-day tournament is expected to provide a boost to various sectors, including lodging, sports betting, and media companies.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Two Harbors Investment (TWO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TWO]) will vote on the amended merger agreement with CrossCountry Mortgage.
 	* ALL DAY - Nvidia (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]) CEO Jensen Huang has been invited to testify to the Senate about the company's China business and U.S. export controls.
 	* 8:30 P.M. The May PPI report will be released. Economists expect core PPI to be up 0.7% month-over-month.
 	* 4:30 P.M. Notable investor events include Honeywell's (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON]) Investor Day, DXC Technology's (DXC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DXC]) Investor Day, and EnerSys' (ENS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ENS]) Investor Day event.

FRIDAY - JUNE 12

 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Centessa Pharmaceuticals (CNTA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CNTA]) will vote on the acquisition offer from Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]).
 	* ALL DAY - KLA Corporation (KLAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KLAC]) will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis following the 10-for-1 stock split.
 	* ALL DAY - The European Hematology Association Congress will begin in Stockholm. Companies in the spotlight during the four-day event include Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]), Novartis (NVS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVS]), and Roche (RHHBY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RHHBY])/Genentech.
 	* ALL DAY - Steven Spielberg's _Disclosure Day_ will open in U.S. theaters. The Universal (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) film arrives amid rising interest in UFOs. The weekend box office forecast is for a solid +$40M showing.
 	* ALL DAY - An AI-generated film will debut at the Tribeca Film Festival. _Dreams of Violets _is the first feature-length, live-action, AI-generated movie to be shown at a major film festival.

MORE ON THE MARKETS

title: SpaceX IPO Orders Exceed Supply In $75 Billion Listing Push
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

SpaceX is moving toward what could become the biggest initial public offering ever, with investor demand already running ahead of the shares available, according to people familiar with the matter. After one-on-one meetings with institutional investors, Elon Musk's rocket, satellite and artificial intelligence company has reportedly seen its $75 billion IPO become oversubscribed. The Starbase, Texas-based company is offering about 555.6 million shares at $135 each, a deal that could value SpaceX at roughly $1.8 trillion.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 7 Warning Signs with JPM. Is SPY fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The size of the offering could put SpaceX well ahead of Saudi Aramco's $29.4 billion listing in 2019, potentially more than doubling the current record. The deal is expected to price on June 11 and begin trading the following day, though the marketing process remains early, discussions are still ongoing, and details could change. SpaceX's decision to offer shares at a fixed price before order-taking is also unusual for a large US IPO, where companies typically market shares with a price range before setting final terms.

Investor interest could be further supported by potential index-tracking demand after SpaceX goes public. Nasdaq recently changed its rules so SpaceX can join the Nasdaq 100 Index (NASDAQ:QQQ) in just 15 trading days, while FTSE Russell shortened its waiting period to five trading days. S&P Dow Jones Indices, however, kept its existing eligibility rules for benchmarks including the S&P 500 (SPY). Goldman Sachs Group Inc., Morgan Stanley, Bank of America Corp., Citigroup Inc. and JPMorgan Chase & Co. are leading the deal, with 18 other banks participating, as SpaceX prepares to debut on Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas under the symbol SPCX.

title: Elon Musk poised to be world's first trillionaire
content:
Elon Musk is poised to become the world's first trillionaire when SpaceX (SPAX.PVT) stock debuts next week, with the company's target IPO price valuing his stake at a considerable $866.5 billion on paper.

On Wednesday, SpaceX said in a filing that it will seek proceeds of $75 billion from its initial public offering, a record amount for any IPO. In the filing, the company said it would offer 555,555,555 shares at $135 each. It's an unusual move not to implement a price range and offer a set price, perhaps because demand for SpaceX stock is high.

Musk holds approximately 42% of SpaceX's equity through a dual-class share structure that grants him roughly 82% of voting control via Class B stock. At the $135 IPO price, the updated prospectus values that equity stake at $866.5 billion.

While that amount nearly gets him to $1 trillion, there's also his Tesla (TSLA) stake.

Musk holds approximately 717 million Tesla shares, not including vested stock options, according to the most recent regulatory filings — a position that, at the electric carmaker's current price of roughly $420 per share, is worth approximately $301.1 billion.

Add the two together — $866.5 billion from SpaceX and roughly $301 billion from Tesla — and Musk's stakes in those two companies alone total approximately $1.168 trillion, not including his stakes in Neuralink, the Boring Company, and other investments and assets he may have.

The Bloomberg Billionaires Index, which applies adjustments for lock-up restrictions and other constraints on his ability to liquidate shares, places his net worth at approximately $988 billion at the $135 IPO price, just below the trillion-dollar threshold. On that measure, any appreciation in SpaceX shares above the IPO level when trading opens June 12 would push Musk past the mark.SpaceX chief engineer Elon Musk takes part in a news conference at the SpaceX Starbase, in Brownsville, Texas, on Aug. 25, 2022. (Reuters/Adrees Latif)·REUTERS / REUTERS

SpaceX will list under the ticker SPCX. The company has grown rapidly on the back of Starlink, its satellite internet service, which now serves millions of paying subscribers globally and generates the majority of the company's revenue.

SpaceX has continued to post annual losses as it invests in Starship, its next-generation heavy-lift rocket, and with its AI holdings from its merger with Musk's xAI. Capital expenditures have surged over the past year, as xAI spends heavily on data centers like Colossus and Colossus II.

But investors appear to be looking past red flags like cash burn and corporate governance issues like a dual-class stock structure, with Musk's vision in the forefront — and a big payoff for him on the way.

Story Continues

The final IPO price is expected to be set on the night of Thursday, June 11, with trading beginning the next day on the Nasdaq Composite (^IXIC).

Dive deeper into the SpaceX IPO

The public debut of Elon Musk's rocket and AI company is expected to make history.

title: How to invest in Elon Musk’s SpaceX IPO – and whether you should
content:
Illustration

Investors hope every stock they pick will go “to the moon”, but some companies take that more literally than others.

Elon Musk’s rocket company SpaceX offers UK retail investors the opportunity to own a slice of one of the most widely anticipated stock exchange floats in recent years and gain exposure to technology that spans space travel, artificial intelligence (AI) and high speed internet.

In a first for UK retail investors, individuals holding investment accounts will have an opportunity to participate in the US initial public offering (IPO), an event that has been historically off limits to the UK retail base.

Here, Telegraph Money explains how you can invest in SpaceX and whether the firm is worth the hype.

When is the IPO?

The company is expected to start trading on the tech-heavy Nasdaq stock index on June 12.

SpaceX triggered the IPO process in May when it registered for a listing under the stock market ticker ‘SPCX’, a shorthand which will allow investors to identify the stock on respective platforms.

Mr Musk is offering up to 3pc of his company in the IPO, which equates to an expected capital raise of $75bn (£56bn) and would make it the largest IPO ever.

This would value SpaceX at $1.8trn, an outcome that would launch the Musk to a position of the world’s first trillionaire, thanks to his 42pc stake in the company, alongside his other ventures.

In the past, UK retail investors have been excluded from US IPOs because of strict rules from the US financial regulator, the Securities and Exchange Commission, about the marketing and selling of securities before IPO.

But with SpaceX seeking a blockbuster valuation, the investor base has been broadened ahead of the float in an attempt to tap into Musk’s large cult following.

What are the costs?

Investors will need to fork out $135 per SpaceX share ahead of the IPO.

The minimum investment amount ranges from £500 to £1000 across various investment platforms, but some brokers such as Trading 212 will offer fractional shares for customers who prefer to stake a smaller amount.

Most platforms will waive share dealing fees for investors participating in the IPO. Once shares are held in accounts, however, standard annual management and dealing fees will apply.

These annual management charges range from free to 0.35pc depending on the provider, but fees could be capped for investors with bigger portfolios, depending on the Isa chosen.

Foreign exchange (FX) charges will not be applied at IPO across most platforms. Retail clients with an interactive investor account who apply for the IPO in pounds rather than dollars will have a 0.1pc foreign exchange fee levied on their investment.

Story Continues

Post-IPO, FX charges will occur and dealing fees will also be added for investors buying shares on the secondary market.

How do I invest?

Through a stocks and shares Isa, lifetime Isa, Sipp or general investment account, retail investors will be able to gain access to the so-called “book building” process.

Traditionally, this process allows investment banks to assess market appetite for a stock before it floats on the stock market.

Major platforms including Hargreaves Lansdown, AJ Bell, interactive investor and eToro will provide customers with a chance to participate, and retail investors must have enough cash within their chosen account to cover their intended outlay.

Marex Financial, which owns the broker Winterflood, will act as the middleman in the process. As the “public offer platform operator”, it will provide UK investment platforms and brokers with access to the IPO and allow retail investors to purchase shares.

Investors can also gain indirect exposure to SpaceX by owning shares of investment trusts that hold the company, which are able to take stakes in private companies.

The largest of these is the £17.2bn Scottish Mortgage investment trust, managed by Baillie Gifford, which bought into the company in 2018. It has since seen its $200m stake swell into $3.3bn, a return of more than 1,500pc.

Edinburgh Worldwide, Baillie Gifford US Growth and Schiehallion, also Baillie Gifford-managed trusts, have stakes in SpaceX, too.

RIT Capital Partners is the only other UK-domiciled investment trust with a SpaceX holding.

Should you invest?

Proponents of SpaceX point to its global dominance in space-centric infrastructure and low-cost space travel, having reduced the cost of launching a rocket by more than 95pc per kilogram.

The success story of electric vehicle giant Tesla, another business venture of Mr Musk, has also boosted prospects for SpaceX.

Tom Slater, manager of Scottish Mortgage, recently said in the trust’s final results: “SpaceX is not just building a connectivity business. It is positioning itself at the intersection of launch, energy and AI in a way that no other company on earth can replicate.”

But amid the fervour over owning a chunk of one of the world’s most future-facing companies, questions have been raised about whether it’s worth the price or risk for retail investors.

At a valuation of $1.8tn, SpaceX would slot into the top 10 largest companies globally, but this valuation has inflated significantly over the course of only a few years.

From December 2023 to 2025, SpaceX’s value soared from $180bn to $800bn. Should the IPO go ahead as expected, it would mean $1tn has been added to the company’s value since the end of 2025.

As it stands, though, Starlink is the only profitable division of the SpaceX business. It uses a network of satellites to provide high-speed broadband around the planet.

Analysts at Morningstar expect the profitability of Starlink to offset the research and development costs of SpaceX, but said a valuation of around $780bn, less than half the IPO value, is more consistent with the company’s underlying figures.

The shares are likely to be “overvalued in almost any scenario” as a result, the analysts said.

Joakim Agerback, lead portfolio manager of the Finserve Global Security fund, has decided to avoid the SpaceX hype and warned that retail investors could be poised for a “rocky ride”.

The $1.8tn figure “leaves little room for anything short of exceptional execution and total dominance in the sector for a long period of time”, Mr Agerback added.

Darius McDermott, managing director at Chelsea Financial Services, cautioned that “a lot of the value has gone” due to the skyrocketing expectations for the company over the past couple of years.

While SpaceX represents “a very exciting story”, for retail investors keen to cash in on the company’s rapid growth, “that ship has sailed”, Mr McDermott said.

What are the alternatives?

Once the firm hits the Nasdaq exchange, it is anticipated that it will be added to the S&P 500. Retail investors who own index funds that track the US flagship index or ‘All World’ indices will consequently also reap the rewards if SpaceX’s growth continues apace.

But SpaceX is not the only option for investors who want to explore the space economy.

One of the firm’s biggest competitors is Rocket Lab, which is listed on the Nasdaq and produces everything from rockets to space software. It has delivered impressive returns over the past year, with shares up more than 320pc.

Alternatively, investors could look at aerospace and defence firms. BAE Systems has some exposure to space, for example through its Ascent spacecraft, introduced in April.

Meanwhile, space-themed funds include VanEck’s Space Innovators ETF, which holds Rocket Lab and satellite manufacturer AST SpaceMobile among its top positions, and investment trust Seraphim Space.

The value of Seraphim’s portfolio rose 30.7pc in the three months to the end of March thanks to growing excitement around space technologies. Its biggest holding is Finnish satellite player ICEYE, which comprises 47pc of the trust’s net asset value (Nav).

View Comments

title: SpaceX Excludes China and Hong Kong Investors From Planned $75 Billion IPO (SPCX)
content:
spacex dragon moon NEW SIZE ©Kevein Gill

SpaceX (NASDAQ:SPCX) has reportedly directed the banks managing its planned $75 billion initial public offering to reject investment orders from individuals and organizations based in mainland China and Hong Kong, according to a Bloomberg News report citing people familiar with the matter.

The move comes ahead of what is expected to be the largest stock market debut ever, highlighting the growing regulatory and geopolitical considerations surrounding high-profile U.S. technology listings.

Website Access Restricted in Key Asian Markets

On Friday, SpaceX’s website became inaccessible from internet addresses located in Hong Kong and Shanghai, further signaling the company’s efforts to limit participation from investors in those jurisdictions.

The restrictions coincide with preparations for the IPO and reflect heightened scrutiny around ownership and access to strategically sensitive businesses.

Defense Regulations Cited as Key Factor

According to the report, banks involved in the offering pointed to U.S. International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) as the primary reason for excluding investors from China and Hong Kong.

ITAR governs the transfer and export of defense-related technologies, information and services, and can impose strict compliance obligations on companies operating in sectors with national security implications.

Given SpaceX’s extensive involvement in satellite communications, launch services and government-related contracts, adherence to these regulations is considered a critical element of the offering process.

Largest IPO on Record Expected

The planned flotation is expected to raise approximately $75 billion and could become the largest initial public offering ever completed.

The transaction is anticipated to value SpaceX among the world’s most valuable publicly traded companies and represents one of the most closely watched listings in recent years.

Part of a Broader Shift in U.S. Technology Markets

The reported restrictions reflect a broader trend among American technology and artificial intelligence companies, many of which have become increasingly reluctant to accept Chinese investment.

Growing concerns over national security, intellectual property protection and data governance have prompted companies and regulators to take a more cautious approach toward foreign capital from certain jurisdictions.

Marked Change From Previous Investment Trends

The development contrasts sharply with investment patterns seen over the past decade, when Chinese venture capital firms and private equity investors were active participants in funding Silicon Valley startups and emerging technology companies.

Story Continues

As geopolitical tensions and regulatory scrutiny have increased, access to Chinese capital has become more limited for many U.S. firms operating in strategically important industries.

The reported SpaceX decision underscores how national security considerations are increasingly shaping the structure and investor base of major technology offerings.

SpaceX IPO

title: SpaceX IPO Pitch Targets $1.8 Trillion Valuation On 100x AI Growth
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

SpaceX is asking investors to underwrite one of the biggest growth stories the market has ever seen. Wall Street research teams are pitching a future where the company's artificial intelligence business could grow 100 times by the end of the decade, helping support a targeted $1.8 trillion valuation. The Starbase, Texas-based company has started marketing a $75 billion initial public offering at a fixed price of $135 per share, with pricing expected on June 11 and shares set to trade on the Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) and Nasdaq Texas under the symbol SPCX.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 3 Warning Sign with NDAQ. Is NDAQ fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The center of the pitch is AI. Evercore ISI analysts reportedly expect SpaceX's AI division to reach $755 billion in sales by 2031, up from $3.2 billion last year, while total revenue could top $1 trillion that year after posting $18.7 billion in 2025. Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS) is said to forecast total revenue of $474 billion in 2030, with AI revenue rising roughly 100 times to nearly $322 billion. Evercore's 2030 model reportedly points to $486 billion of total revenue and $331 billion from AI.

The investment case could come with massive spending before the payoff arrives. Goldman's team reportedly sees free cash flow improving to more than $72 billion in 2031 after falling to negative $105 billion in 2029. Evercore's model suggests AI could rise from less than one-fifth of revenue to 74% by 2031, while the space business could fall to 1% of total revenue from more than 20% last year. Both firms expect connectivity sales to exceed $140 billion in 2030 from about $11.4 billion last year, while the rocket division could reach about $8 billion in 2030, roughly double last year's $4.1 billion. Capital expenditures are expected to rise above $360 billion in 2030 from more than $20 billion last year, with Evercore projecting $732 billion of spending in 2031, including roughly $666 billion tied to AI.

title: SpaceX (SPAX) Stock Faces A $780 Billion Reality Check Before IPO
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

Morningstar has assigned Space Exploration Technologies an estimated value of $780b, which is well below the $1.75t valuation the company is reportedly seeking in its upcoming IPO. Analysts at the firm highlight uncertainties around SpaceX's artificial intelligence efforts, orbital data center plans and the Starlink broadband business as key risks. The IPO is expected to list SpaceX under the ticker SPCX on the Nasdaq, with a low float and strong underwriting cited as potential drivers of early trading volatility.

Space Exploration Technologies is preparing for a high profile Nasdaq listing under SPCX, with private market expectations reportedly far above Morningstar's $780b assessment. The current share price reference for the company is $135.0, giving prospective investors a concrete starting point as they assess how proposed valuations compare with analyst views. The gap between internal targets and external estimates is likely to be a focal point during the IPO roadshow.

For readers watching SpaceX stock ahead of the listing, the key questions center on how the market prices Starlink's growth potential, the feasibility of orbital data centers and the economics of the xAI and Grok businesses. Short term trading could be shaped by limited share availability and strong bank support. Longer term pricing is more likely to track progress on these core projects and the revenue streams they eventually deliver.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Space Exploration Technologies by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Space Exploration Technologies.SPCX Revenue & Expenses Breakdown as at June 2026

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Space Exploration Technologies following this latest news.

The valuation gap between Morningstar's US$780b estimate and the reported US$1.75t IPO target sets up a clear tension for prospective Space Exploration Technologies investors. On one side is strong enthusiasm for access to SpaceX stock, supported by a low-float structure and heavyweight underwriters that could support early trading strength. On the other side is a fundamental view that a large portion of the IPO price hinges on unproven businesses, particularly AI projects tied to xAI and X, orbital data center concepts and the long-term economics of Starlink. For readers, this is a reminder that high-profile offerings can attract momentum-focused buyers early on, while more valuation-conscious investors may wait to see how pricing settles once initial demand normalizes.

Story Continues

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

⚠️ A major risk is that shares may be highly illiquid around the IPO, with low float and concentrated demand potentially leading to sharp price swings. ⚠️ Morningstar highlights uncertainty around SpaceX's AI plans, orbital data centers and Starlink's technological hurdles, which could affect how much value the market ultimately assigns to these projects. 🎁 Enthusiasm for the listing and a strong group of underwriters, including several large investment banks, could support robust initial demand for Space Exploration Technologies stock. 🎁 The company has been growing profit or revenue, with revenue up 27.3% over the past year, which provides a fundamental backdrop that some long-term investors may focus on as the post-IPO trading range develops.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, investors watching Space Exploration Technologies should focus on how the market reconciles the US$1.75t target with Morningstar's US$780b estimate once trading begins. Pricing behavior in the first few weeks, including liquidity and intraday volatility, will offer clues about whether demand is dominated by short-term traders or longer-horizon holders. Over time, updates on Starlink's network performance and profitability, clarity on xAI and Grok monetization and any concrete progress on orbital data centers will be key markers for whether the IPO valuation is being supported by business outcomes or relies more on expectation.

To ensure you're always in the loop on how the latest news impacts the investment narrative for Space Exploration Technologies, head to the  community page for Space Exploration Technologies to never miss an update on the top community narratives.

title: SpaceX, Other Megacap IPOs Won't Get S&P 500 Fast Track
content:
S&P Dow Jones Indices rejected calls to fast track SpaceX and other upcoming megacap IPOs such as Anthropic and OpenAI into the S&P 500 index.
